summary: Improved matching in gmresult by reshaping local variables, stack padding, direct global accesses, color assignment style, and one language-dependent resource filename. The PR also split a formerly single data symbol at 0x803D68CC into smaller SJIS/string symbols in GALE01 symbols.txt, which likely helped data references and string boundaries line up with the original binary.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, lbl_803D68CC changed from one 0x34-byte object into several smaller symbols: lbl_803D68CC as size 0x9 data:sjis, plus lbl_803D68D8, lbl_803D68E4, and lbl_803D68F4 as separate data:string entries.
In fn_80174284, removed PAD_STACK(8), introduced a temporary struct UnkResultPlayerData* pointing to lbl_8046DBE8.x94->x44C, and indexed tmp[slot].x0[i] directly instead of using a u8* buf local.

In fn_8017507C, reordered locals by adding an explicit u8 _[8] before GXColor color, removed unnecessary u8 casts on is_teams and slot_type comparisons, changed gm_80160968 arguments from unsigned constants like 4U to plain integer constants, and collapsed white RGBA assignment into color.r = color.g = color.b = color.a = 0xFF.
In fn_80175C5C, added an explicit u8 _[4] local and reduced PAD_STACK from 8 to 4, adjusting the apparent stack layout around Vec3 position.

In fn_80175D34, removed a ResultsData* data alias to lbl_8046DBE8, added u8 _[12], reduced PAD_STACK from 20 to 8, accessed lbl_8046DBE8.player_data directly for ko_count and ko_time, and kept ko_time as a local only where needed.
In fn_80176A6C, changed the non-US language branch to load "SdRst.dat" instead of "SdRst.usd" while the US branch still loads "SdRst.usd".
smart_moves: Replacing generic PAD_STACK-only adjustments with explicit dummy byte arrays such as u8 _[8], u8 _[4], and u8 _[12] made the intended stack layout more controllable while preserving source-level readability.
Using a typed pointer to struct UnkResultPlayerData in fn_80174284 avoided a separate byte-buffer local and expressed the original indexed access pattern more directly.

Directly referencing lbl_8046DBE8.player_data instead of carrying a ResultsData* alias helped the generated code match better in fn_80175D34, suggesting the original compiler did not keep that alias in the same way.
Splitting a broad data object into correctly typed SJIS/string symbols gave the linker/decompiler more accurate data boundaries, which is often necessary for nearby string references to match.
The non-US resource filename correction to "SdRst.dat" matches the apparent language branch intent and may also avoid misleading duplicate string references to "SdRst.usd".
decomp_lessons: For gmresult-style matching, small source-level choices around aliases, temporary pointers, and direct global access can materially affect stack usage and register allocation.
Do not assume a broad anonymous data object is correct when adjacent embedded strings exist; split symbols at exact string/SJIS boundaries when the data layout supports it.
Explicit dummy byte arrays can be a useful alternative or supplement to PAD_STACK when trying to reproduce a specific stack frame shape.
Collapsing repeated scalar assignments into a chained assignment can alter emitted code enough to improve a match.
Removing unnecessary casts and unsigned suffixes can help recover the compiler's original expression form, especially for comparisons against byte-like struct fields and small switch arguments.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions in this slice retain address-based names such as fn_80174284, fn_8017507C, fn_80175C5C, fn_80175D34, and fn_80176A6C.
Unidentified data labels retain lbl_ address-style names such as lbl_8046DBE8 and lbl_803D68CC.
Dummy stack filler locals are named with underscore-only identifiers, e.g. u8 _[8], u8 _[4], and u8 _[12].
Typed data symbols in symbols.txt use comments such as data:sjis and data:string to document expected object contents.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use typed struct pointer temporaries when byte-pointer aliases produce worse codegen.
Remove local aliases to globals when the original code likely accessed the global directly.
Tune stack layout with a combination of explicit byte arrays and PAD_STACK size changes.
Try chained assignments for repeated identical field initialization when matching store ordering or register usage.
Split data symbols into precise SJIS/string entries to improve reference resolution and adjacent data matching.
Prefer plain integer constants over unsigned-suffixed constants when matching function call argument codegen.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but the provided human_text_excerpt is empty, so no commenter feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename gmresult functions fn_80174284, fn_8017507C, fn_80175D34, or fn_80176A6C.
Search for additional symbols.txt splits around lbl_803D68CC or nearby gmresult string tables.
Check whether the "SdRst.dat" non-US branch was validated or further changed in later language/resource PRs.
Find other gmresult matching PRs that use explicit u8 _[] dummy locals to tune stack frames.
